mapping.a_element: affine Lie algebra (SU(3)) and its modular invariant partition functions
mapping.b_element: simple Lie algebras and their associated quantum invariants of 3-manifolds

mapping.a_element: classification of modular invariant partition functions for SU(3)
mapping.b_element: decomposition of quantum invariants into projective and non-projective parts

mapping.a_element: modular invariance as a symmetry principle in conformal field theory
mapping.b_element: symmetry principle used to decompose quantum invariants

brief_justification: While both papers involve simple Lie algebras and symmetry principles, Paper A focuses on classifying modular invariant partition functions in conformal field theory, whereas Paper B addresses the decomposition and integrality of quantum invariants of 3-manifolds. The core mechanisms—classification via modular invariance vs. decomposition and perturbative expansion of quantum invariants—are distinct.
